regrade_note: Re-grade after 6 targeted bug fixes (4 code logic + 2 UI). Original score: 48. Fixes resolved all 4 original correctness failures, 2 UI quality issues, and 2 spec fidelity failures.

correctness.justification: All 4 original correctness failures are now resolved. COR-2 (runtime TypeErrors): Fix 3 added null guards (.filter() and optional chaining) to all affected computed bodies; manual-test-002 confirms no TypeErrors during piece step on fresh piece. COR-3 (submitRequest always showing Denied): Fix 2 pre-computes allocation result using allocateSpot() before calling requestParking.send(); critic-003 confirmed state snapshots are consistent with no divergence risk in CT's single-threaded model; manual-test-002 verified all 3 message scenarios (success, denial, duplicate). COR-4 (week-ahead grid invisible rows): Fix 5 removed overflowX auto wrapper and added tableLayout:fixed; manual-test-003 confirmed all 3 spot rows render with visible height after scroll. COR-5 (INITIAL_SPOTS not seeded): Fix 1 changed input default to Default<ParkingSpot[], typeof INITIAL_SPOTS>; critic-003 confirmed this is the idiomatic CT seeding mechanism (matching card-piles.tsx exemplar); manual-test-002 confirmed spots #1, #5, #12 visible on first load. COR-1 (compilation) and COR-6 (TypeScript errors) were already passing and remain clean. Remaining cosmetic issues (date display wrapping, placeholder text in forms) do not affect correctness.

idiomaticity.justification: Unchanged from original score. Critic-003 confirmed no regressions introduced by any of the 6 fixes. All 13 applicable checks pass: action() for all pattern-scope handlers, no handler()/lift() inside pattern body, all derived values in computed(), Default<> on all array inputs, Writable<> correctly typed, Stream<T> for outputs, [NAME] and [UI] present, $value/$checked bindings correct throughout, object-style syntax on HTML elements / string-style on ct-* elements, camelCase event names correct, no Map/Set in cell data, no Stream.of()/.subscribe(), no async/await in handlers. IDI-8 is N/A (no LLM used). Fix 1's new type annotation Default<ParkingSpot[], typeof INITIAL_SPOTS> is confirmed idiomatic CT usage.

failures.description: 10 inline arrow functions created per-item inside .map() loops remain unchanged. Critic-003 confirmed the count is identical to critic-002 — no regression, but no fix either. Violations: (1) cancelRequest in myRequests.map() at line 1162, (2) movePriorityUp in priorityList.map() at line 1208, (3) movePriorityDown at line 1215, (4) removePerson at line 1222, (5) openEditPerson at line 1223, (6) openEditSpot in spots.map() at line 1289, (7) removeSpot at line 1296, (8) movePrefUp in editPersonPrefDetails.map() at line 1464, (9) movePrefDown at line 1471, (10) removePrefSpot at line 1478. Per rubric: -5 per handler created inside .map(). 10 violations × -5 = -50 deduction.
failures.suggested_fix: Extract module-scope handler() instances for each per-item operation. For example: const handleCancelRequest = handler<void, { requestId: string }>((_, { requestId }) => { ... }); then in .map(): onClick={handleCancelRequest({ requestId: r.id })}. Since module-scope handlers cannot close over pattern-scope actions, the action logic must be moved into the handler body or the pattern refactored so core logic lives at module scope.

reactivity.justification: Unchanged from original score. All major reactivity checks pass: no stale .get() on computeds, no Writable.of(reactive), no inline array operations in JSX, no handler()/lift() inside pattern body. The single failure is REA-9: 10 inline arrow functions created per-item in .map() loops, applying -5 per instance for a total -50 deduction, yielding a score of 50. This was a consistent design decision carried through all 3 critic passes and not addressed in the fix rounds. REA-8 is N/A (lift() not used). REA-5 passes trivially ([NAME] is a static string literal).

failures.description: Date display in My Requests renders across 3 lines ('2026-' / '02-' / '24'), affecting readability of request history entries. The column width for the date field is too narrow for the ISO date string format. This was Issue 7 (LOW severity) in manual-test-002 and manual-test-003 (out of scope for retesting). The original admin overflow issue (UIQ-4 in original score) was fixed by Fix 6; this is a different remaining readability concern in the same check.
failures.suggested_fix: Ensure the My Requests date column has a minimum width of at least 7em to display YYYY-MM-DD on a single line, or format the date as a shorter locale string (e.g., '2/24/26') to fit in a narrower column.

failures.description: The active tab indicator logic was not modified in either fix round. Fix 6 correctly hides admin tabs (People/Spots) on initial load and restores the 2-tab non-admin nav bar cleanly, but did not address the underlying active-state computation for the Spots tab. When admin mode is active and the user navigates to the Today or My Requests view, the Spots tab may still show as highlighted blue (the active state was driven by a computed that may not correctly track currentView). Manual-test-003 confirmed the overflow fix but did not re-verify the Spots tab active state during navigation.
failures.suggested_fix: Audit the variant attribute on the admin navigation tab buttons. Ensure each tab's variant is computed as: variant={computed(() => currentView.get() === 'admin-spots' ? 'primary' : 'ghost')}. The tab should only show 'primary' variant when its associated view is the current view.

ui_quality.justification: UI quality score is unchanged at 90, but for different reasons than the original. Fix 6 resolved the original UIQ-4 failure (severe admin toggle overflow confirmed fixed in manual-test-003 with 2 consecutive toggle cycles) and as a bonus also resolved Issue 5 (admin tabs correctly hidden on initial load). Fix 5 resolved the week-ahead grid invisible rows issue. However, two -5 deductions remain: UIQ-4 (-5) for date display wrapping in My Requests (3-line rendering of ISO date strings, LOW severity but a readable text sizes concern per the rubric), and UIQ-6 (-5) for the Spots tab active-state logic which was not targeted by either fix round and remains potentially incorrect when admin mode is active. All other UI checks pass: ct-* components used throughout, $value/$checked bindings correct, ct-screen with slots used, no <option> in ct-select, empty states handled.

failures.description: 4 tests continue to fail when running ct test (60 passed, 4 failed). The failures are: (1) action_6 (editSpot) times out after 5000ms due to same-length array replacement via .set(toSpliced()) not reliably triggering reactive change detection after .push() has been called on the same Writable; (2) assertion_19 (spot1 label = 'Covered') cascade failure from action_6 timeout; (3) assertion_20 (spot1 notes = 'Near lobby') cascade failure; (4) assertion_63 (charlie manual override spot-12) intermittent failure due to insufficient propagation warmup. These are characterized as test harness reactive detection limitations rather than pattern logic bugs — the editSpot action was verified correct in manual testing — but the rubric applies the cap mechanically: failing tests = cap at 30.
failures.suggested_fix: For editSpot test: run the test in isolation before any .push() calls, or restructure editSpot to use a completely new array reference (not a splice-based mutation) to make changes reliably detectable by the test harness. For manualOverride: increase propagation warmup assertions to ensure sufficient cycles after multiple preceding state mutations. These are test file changes, not pattern logic changes.

test_coverage.justification: TST-2 continues to trigger the critical cap at 30. The test count improved from 59/5 (pipeline.json original) to 60/4 (post-fix) — one additional test passes, but 4 still fail. All 4 failures are attributed to test harness reactive detection limitations (same-length array mutation detection, propagation cycle timing) rather than pattern logic errors. The rubric applies the cap mechanically regardless of root cause. Within the cap, coverage quality remains strong: TST-1 PASS (test file exists with 64 tests), TST-3 PASS (non-trivial state transitions thoroughly tested), TST-4 PASS (pattern-as-test idiom used correctly throughout), TST-5 PASS (edge cases covered including duplicate prevention, cascade operations, denial scenarios).

code_quality.justification: Unchanged from original score. Critic-003 regression check confirmed no code quality regressions from any of the 6 fixes. Types remain clearly named after domain concepts (ParkingSpot, Person, SpotRequest, CommuteMode, RequestStatus). Single-file structure appropriate for intermediate complexity. No over-abstraction: allocateSpot remains a pure module-scope helper. All helpers at module scope. Consistent naming throughout. Normalized state with ID-based references. Unidirectional data flow. The null guard additions (Fix 3) and CSS constraint additions (Fix 6) are clean and non-disruptive to code structure.

failures.description: Past-date prevention remains only at submit time, not at form level. The spec states: 'The form does not allow selecting past dates. Only today and future dates (up to 30 days ahead) are selectable.' The date ct-input (around line 1079/1093) does not have min or max attributes constraining the selectable range. Submit-time validation exists (if date < TODAY return with error message) but the form itself allows typing or selecting past dates. Critic-003 Standing Priority 3 notes this as unchanged. No fix was applied to address this.
failures.suggested_fix: Add min={TODAY} and max={getDateOffset(30)} attributes to the date ct-input element. This enforces the date range constraint at the form level as the spec requires, preventing invalid input rather than only rejecting it on submit.

spec_fidelity.justification: Significant improvement from original score of 70. SPF-1 (FIXED): Initial spots now correctly seeded via Default<ParkingSpot[], typeof INITIAL_SPOTS>; the first acceptance criterion (spots #1, #5, #12 visible on first load) is fully met. SPF-2 (PASS, unchanged): Data model matches spec — ParkingSpot, Person, SpotRequest with all required fields, separate priorityOrder list. SPF-4 (FIXED): The pattern is no longer incomplete for its intended use case. Manual-test-003 reports 17/19 acceptance criteria pass with 2 partial passes for low-severity cosmetic items; the core user journey (request parking, get correct result, view it in today panel and week-ahead grid) works end-to-end. SPF-3 (FAIL, -5): Form-level date range constraint still missing; spec explicitly requires the form not to allow past date selection, but only submit-time validation is present.

overall.summary: The targeted fixes delivered substantial improvement: correctness rose from 20 to 100 (all 4 original failures resolved — TypeErrors eliminated, request message corrected, week-ahead grid visible, INITIAL_SPOTS seeded), and spec fidelity rose from 70 to 95 (initial spots and pattern completeness both resolved). The raw weighted score is 84 before the process efficiency modifier. However, two structural issues persist across all fix rounds: REA-9 (10 inline arrow functions in .map() loops, -50 deduction in reactivity, reducing that dimension to 50) and TST-2 (4 test harness failures cap test coverage at 30). The -15 process efficiency modifier (6 pipeline build iterations: 5 build cycles + 1 fix pass) reduces the final score to 69 — one point below the acceptable threshold of 70. To clear the 70 threshold, at minimum the TST-2 harness failures must be resolved, or both TST-2 and REA-9 partially addressed to offset the process efficiency penalty.
